title: 1.1: Changes the `Logger` field type in `TestPreInitState` struct within `lib/test_state.go` from `*logrus.Logger` to `logrus.FieldLogger`

annotations.description: Verifies that TestPreInitState.Logger uses the logrus.FieldLogger interface type instead of a concrete logger.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 146-156 (lib/test_state.go): lines 150-152 remove the TODO comment and `Logger *logrus.Logger`, line 153 adds `Logger logrus.FieldLogger`.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states the Logger fields in lib/test_state.go use concrete *logrus.Logger with TODO comments acknowledging they should use logrus.FieldLogger, and asks to refactor to use interfaces. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The interface specifies TestPreInitState.Logger with output type logrus.FieldLogger at path lib/test_state.go. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct and good answer must change TestPreInitState.Logger from *logrus.Logger to logrus.FieldLogger in lib/test_state.go.

title: 1.3: Adds a `NewLogHook` function in `lib/testutils/logrus_hook.go` that accepts variadic `logrus.Level` arguments and returns a `*SimpleLogrusHook`, defaulting to all levels when none are provided

annotations.description: Verifies a reusable hook constructor exists with the prescribed signature to reduce test boilerplate.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 195-201 (lib/testutils/logrus_hook.go): lines 196-201 add the NewLogHook function that defaults to logrus.AllLevels when no levels are passed.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt asks for hook construction with configurable log levels that default to all levels to reduce boilerplate. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The interface specifies NewLogHook(levels ...logrus.Level) returning *SimpleLogrusHook at path lib/testutils/logrus_hook.go. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct and good answer must add a NewLogHook function with variadic levels and all-levels default in lib/testutils/logrus_hook.go.

title: 1.4: Adds a `Reset` method on `SimpleLogrusHook` in `lib/testutils/logrus_hook.go` that clears captured entries without returning them

annotations.description: Verifies a hook reset capability exists with the prescribed signature to support reuse between subtests.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 173-178 (lib/testutils/logrus_hook.go): lines 174-178 add the Reset method that locks the mutex and clears messageCache.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt asks for clearing captured entries for reuse between subtests. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The interface specifies SimpleLogrusHook.Reset() with no parameters and no return at path lib/testutils/logrus_hook.go. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct and good answer must add a Reset method on SimpleLogrusHook that clears captured entries in lib/testutils/logrus_hook.go.

title: 1.5: Adds a `LastEntry` method on `SimpleLogrusHook` in `lib/testutils/logrus_hook.go` that returns the most recent captured `*logrus.Entry` or nil if none exist

annotations.description: Verifies a convenience accessor exists with the prescribed signature for inspecting the most recent log entry.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 180-190 (lib/testutils/logrus_hook.go): lines 181-189 add the LastEntry method that returns the last element of messageCache or nil.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt asks for inspecting the most recent entry directly without draining the full buffer. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The interface specifies SimpleLogrusHook.LastEntry() returning *logrus.Entry at path lib/testutils/logrus_hook.go. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct and good answer must add a LastEntry method on SimpleLogrusHook returning *logrus.Entry or nil in lib/testutils/logrus_hook.go.

title: 1.6: Changes the return type of `NewLogger` in `lib/testutils/test_output.go` from `*logrus.Logger` to `logrus.FieldLogger`

annotations.description: Verifies the primary test logger utility returns an interface type compatible with interface-based logger fields.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 229-255 (lib/testutils/test_output.go): line 253 declares `func NewLogger(t testing.TB) logrus.FieldLogger` replacing the old concrete return type.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states existing helpers return concrete types rather than interfaces and asks that logger creation helpers return interface types. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The interface specifies NewLogger(t testing.TB) returning logrus.FieldLogger at path lib/testutils/test_output.go. [3: Logical Necessity] Given the Logger fields are now logrus.FieldLogger, the helper that creates loggers for tests must return the matching interface type to avoid unnecessary type assertions at every call site. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct and good answer must change NewLogger to return logrus.FieldLogger in lib/testutils/test_output.go.

title: 1.7: Adds a `NewLoggerWithHook` function in `lib/testutils/test_output.go` that accepts `t testing.TB` and variadic `logrus.Level` arguments, returning both a `logrus.FieldLogger` and a `*SimpleLogrusHook`

annotations.description: Verifies a single-call helper exists with the prescribed signature to create a logger and hook together.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 257-277 (lib/testutils/test_output.go): lines 260-277 add NewLoggerWithHook that creates a logger, attaches a hook, and returns both.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt asks for a convenient single call to get both a logger and a hook together. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The interface specifies NewLoggerWithHook(t testing.TB, levels ...logrus.Level) returning (logrus.FieldLogger, *SimpleLogrusHook) at path lib/testutils/test_output.go. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct and good answer must add NewLoggerWithHook with the prescribed signature in lib/testutils/test_output.go.

title: 1.10: Replaces the direct `r.preInitState.Logger.Formatter` access in `Runner.SetOptions` in `js/runner.go` with a type assertion that extracts the `logrus.Formatter` through the `logrus.FieldLogger` interface

annotations.description: Verifies that the Formatter access in js/runner.go is adapted to work through the interface via a type assertion instead of directly accessing the concrete *logrus.Logger field.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 93-107 (js/runner.go): lines 98-104 replace the direct `r.preInitState.Logger.Formatter` access with a type assertion to extract the formatter safely through the interface.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt explicitly asks to adapt production code that directly accesses concrete logger fields like Formatter to work safely through the interface. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] In the codebase, js/runner.go SetOptions accesses r.preInitState.Logger.Formatter, which is only valid on *logrus.Logger and would fail to compile once the field becomes logrus.FieldLogger. [3: Logical Necessity] Given the Logger field is now an interface, any code accessing concrete-only fields must use type assertions or alternative approaches to compile. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct and good answer must adapt the Formatter access in js/runner.go to work through the logrus.FieldLogger interface.

title: 1.11: Changes the `loadSystemCertPool` function parameter in `cmd/test_load.go` from `*logrus.Logger` to `logrus.FieldLogger`

[2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] loadSystemCertPool only calls WithError().Warning() on the logger, both available on logrus.FieldLogger, so accepting the interface is safe. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct and good answer should change loadSystemCertPool's logger parameter to logrus.FieldLogger in cmd/test_load.go.

title: 1.12: Adds a linter rule in `.golangci.yml` that flags direct usage of the concrete `logrus.Logger` type to prevent regressions

annotations.description: Verifies automated guardrails exist to discourage regressions back to concrete logrus logger usage.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 1-12 (.golangci.yml): line 9 adds a forbidigo pattern `'^logrus\.Logger$'` to the existing list of forbidden identifiers.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt explicitly asks to add a lint rule to prevent regressions back to using the concrete logger type. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The repository already uses golangci-lint with a forbidigo section in .golangci.yml to enforce import/usage restrictions. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct and good answer must add a lint rule in .golangci.yml that flags concrete logrus.Logger usage.

title: 1.13: Removes the `NewMemLogger` function from `lib/testutils/logrus_hook.go` that returned `(*logrus.Logger, *SimpleLogrusHook)` with a concrete logger type

annotations.description: Verifies that the NewMemLogger function, which returned a concrete *logrus.Logger, is removed from lib/testutils/logrus_hook.go since it is superseded by the new interface-returning helpers.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 206-224 (lib/testutils/logrus_hook.go): lines 210-224 remove the NewMemLogger function that returned (*logrus.Logger, *SimpleLogrusHook), eliminating the last public concrete-returning helper.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'The logger creation helpers should return interface types.' [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] lib/testutils/logrus_hook.go has NewMemLogger which returns (*logrus.Logger, *SimpleLogrusHook), a concrete return type that contradicts the prompt requirement. NewLoggerWithHook (1.7) supersedes this function. [3: Logical Necessity] Given the prompt requires helpers to return interfaces, NewMemLogger must be removed since it returns a concrete type. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct and good answer must remove the NewMemLogger function from lib/testutils/logrus_hook.go.

title: 4.1: Removes or changes the signatures of existing `SimpleLogrusHook` methods `Levels`, `Fire`, `Drain`, or `Lines` in `lib/testutils/logrus_hook.go`

annotations.description: Detects regressions where existing hook API methods are broken while adding new ones.
annotations.evidence: gold.patch lines 157-224 (lib/testutils/logrus_hook.go): the patch adds new methods (Reset, LastEntry, NewLogHook) without modifying the existing Levels, Fire, Drain, or Lines methods.
annotations.justification: [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] Existing tests and utilities throughout the codebase depend on Levels, Fire, Drain, and Lines methods of SimpleLogrusHook. Removing or changing them would break consumers. [3: Logical Necessity] Given the prompt asks for additions and the existing methods have dependents, preserving them is mandatory. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct and good answer must preserve the existing SimpleLogrusHook methods unchanged.

title: 4.2: Modifies any existing Go test file (`*_test.go`) as part of the refactoring

annotations.description: Detects violation of the explicit instruction not to modify test files.
annotations.evidence: The prompt explicitly states: 'Do NOT modify any test files (*_test.go) or testing logic in any way. Your task is to make the minimal changes to non-test source files only.'
annotations.justification: [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The test files (*_test.go) are managed separately and already contain adaptations for the new interface signatures via base_test.patch; modifying them would conflict with those pre-applied changes. [3: Logical Necessity] Since the refactoring scope is explicitly limited to non-test source files, any agent touching *_test.go files is operating outside the defined scope. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct and good answer must not modify any *_test.go files.
